content.text: Internal shared libraries follow a defined lifecycle: each major version has an
during which it receives new features, bug fixes, and security patches; then a
after the next major is released; and finally
when the version receives no further updates. The policy tells consuming teams exactly how long they have to migrate before their dependency becomes unsupported.

attrs.alt: Library version lifecycle: active support, security-only window, and end-of-life timeline

content.text: All internal shared libraries follow semantic versioning strictly. The meaning of each component is binding — not a suggestion:
New optional parameter added to a public method
add optional timeout= param to complete()
New public class or module added
Bug fix that does not change the API
fix retry logic that failed to honour Retry-After header
fix JWKS cache that failed to invalidate on key rotation
complete() now returns Response instead of str

A change is only non-breaking if existing call sites compile and behave identically with zero changes. Adding a required parameter is always a major bump even if it has a sensible default — the default is a behavioral change.
Bug fixes, new features (minor/patch), security patches, documentation
90 days after the next major is released
Critical security patches only; no new features or non-security bug fixes
Nothing. Services still on the old major are unsupported and may have unpatched vulnerabilities.
. For libraries with widespread usage across many teams, the platform team may extend it to 180 days — but never shorter. The window is announced in the release notes when the new major ships.
A deprecation is distinct from an EOL: deprecation signals that a specific API element (method, parameter, class) will be removed in the
major version. Deprecations are communicated at least one minor release before the major that removes them.
in the warning text — never just say 'deprecated', say
that removes the deprecated element after at least one minor with the warning active.
documents every removed/renamed item and provides a one-to-one replacement.

that pins the major version and allows automatic minor and patch updates. This is the recommended strategy because minor and patch releases are safe to receive automatically; major releases require explicit opt-in.

# pyproject.toml in a consuming service (Python)
[project]
dependencies = [
    "acme-claude>=1.2.0,<2.0.0",   # receive 1.2.x, 1.3.x, etc. automatically
]
) unless they have a specific, documented reason. Exact pins mean security patches never reach the service automatically.
A weekly CI job scans the dependency manifests of all Claude service repositories and reports which services are lagging behind the latest supported version:
Every release must include a CHANGELOG entry following the
format. The platform team enforces this with a CI lint check that rejects PRs where the CHANGELOG was not updated alongside a version bump.

# CHANGELOG.md

## [Unreleased]

## [1.5.0] — 2026-06-15
### Added
- `session_token_budget` parameter on `ClaudeClient.__init__` to cap total tokens per session.
- `ClaudeClient.tokens_used` property to inspect current session usage.

### Changed
- Default `max_tokens` changed from 2048 to 4096 to match Claude's new recommended default.

## [1.4.0] — 2026-04-02
### Added
- `MockClaudeClient` in `acme_claude.testing` for unit tests.

### Fixed
- Retry logic now correctly honours the `Retry-After` header on rate limit responses.

## [1.3.0] — 2026-01-10
### Deprecated
- `complete_v1(prompt: str)` is deprecated. Use `complete(messages=[...])` instead.
  Will be removed in v2.0.

## [2.0.0] — planned Q3 2026
### Breaking Changes
- `complete_v1()` removed (deprecated since 1.3.0).
- `session_name` parameter removed from constructor (was unused since 1.1.0).
Critical security vulnerabilities trigger an emergency patch outside the normal release cadence. The process:
: CVSS score ≥ 7.0 triggers an emergency release within 24 hours. CVSS ≥ 4.0 within 7 days.
: apply the fix to the current active-support major and any major still in the security-only window.
: post to the internal #platform-announcements Slack channel with CVE ID (if public), affected versions, and the patched version number.
: the propagation CI job opens PRs in every consuming repository bumping the patch version. Teams are expected to merge and deploy within 72 hours for CVSS ≥ 7.0.
: the version drift check script runs after the 72-hour window and flags any services still on the vulnerable version.
Services that use exact-pinned versions (==1.2.0) cannot receive automated patch PRs — the exact pin blocks the update. This is the primary operational reason exact pinning is prohibited.
Every release follows a defined process to ensure quality, traceability, and consistency across the suite of internal libraries. The process is enforced by GitHub Actions and cannot be bypassed.
, add the CHANGELOG entry for the new version.
workflow runs the full test suite, checks for CHANGELOG update, validates that the public API matches type stubs, and runs backwards-compatibility tests against the previous minor version.
git tag -a vX.Y.Z -m 'release message' && git push origin vX.Y.Z
workflow builds the distribution and uploads to the internal PyPI / npm registry.
: post a release note to #platform-announcements with the version, key changes, and migration guidance for breaking changes.

# .github/workflows/release-gate.yml
name: Release gate
on:
  push:
    branches: ["release/v*"]

jobs:
  gate:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0   # needed to compare with previous tag

      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with: {python-version: "3.11"}

      - run: pip install -e .[dev]

      - name: Run tests
        run: pytest --cov=acme_claude --cov-fail-under=90

      - name: Verify CHANGELOG updated
        run: |
          VERSION=$(python -c "import importlib.metadata; print(importlib.metadata.version('acme-claude'))")
          grep -q "## \[$VERSION\]" CHANGELOG.md || (echo "CHANGELOG missing entry for $VERSION"; exit 1)

      - name: Backwards-compat test against previous minor
        run: |
          PREV=$(git tag --sort=-version:refname | grep -v "$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0)" | head -1)
          pip install "acme-claude==$PREV"  # install previous version in a separate venv
          python tests/backwards_compat.py  # test that old call sites still work

Every release runs a backwards compatibility test suite that exercises the API as a consumer would call it, using the call patterns from the previous minor version. This catches accidental breaking changes that bypass semantic version discipline.

# tests/backwards_compat.py  (runs against the PREVIOUS version)
"""
Tests that call the public API in the same way as consumers do.
If these fail after a bump, the change is breaking and needs a major version.
"""
from acme_claude import ClaudeClient
from acme_claude.testing import MockClaudeClient


def test_client_instantiation_no_required_args():
    # Constructor must remain callable with no required arguments
    client = ClaudeClient()
    assert client is not None


def test_complete_accepts_messages_list():
    mock = MockClaudeClient(responses=["hello"])
    result = mock.complete([{"role": "user", "content": "hi"}])
    assert result.content[0].text == "hello"


def test_complete_accepts_keyword_model():
    # model kwarg must remain optional and keyword-passable
    mock = MockClaudeClient(responses=["ok"])
    result = mock.complete(
        [{"role": "user", "content": "hi"}],
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
    )
    assert result is not None


def test_mock_client_records_calls():
    mock = MockClaudeClient(responses=["a", "b"])
    mock.complete([{"role": "user", "content": "first"}])
    mock.complete([{"role": "user", "content": "second"}])
    assert len(mock.calls) == 2
Internal libraries ship inline type annotations (not separate
stub files) and are PEP 561 compliant by including
in the package. This enables mypy and pyright to type-check consumer code without additional configuration.

# pyproject.toml of acme-claude
[project]
name = "acme-claude"
# py.typed marker makes this package typed (PEP 561)
[tool.setuptools.package-data]
acme_claude = ["py.typed"]

[tool.mypy]
strict = true
disallow_untyped_defs = true

[tool.pyright]
strict = ["acme_claude/"]

# acme_claude/client.py — inline types throughout
from __future__ import annotations
import anthropic

class ClaudeClient:
    def complete(
        self,
        messages: list[anthropic.types.MessageParam],
        *,
        model: str | None = None,
        max_tokens: int | None = None,
        system: str | None = None,
    ) -> anthropic.types.Message:
        ...
on the library source and on a representative consumer fixture. A regression in type correctness fails the release gate.
When a major version ships, teams need more than a CHANGELOG entry to migrate. The platform team provides:
: a docs page listing every removed/renamed symbol with its replacement and a code example. The guide follows a strict
format so teams can scan it quickly.
codemod that automatically rewrites the most common call patterns. Not all migrations can be automated, but mechanical renames and parameter reorderings usually can.
: the platform team runs two optional 30-minute office hours sessions in the first two weeks after a major release for teams to ask migration questions.
: a pinned thread in #platform-announcements where teams post migration blockers and get responses within one business day.

# codemods/migrate_v1_to_v2.py (libcst codemod)
"""
Renames complete_v1(prompt) -> complete(messages=[{...}])
Run with: python -m libcst.tool codemod codemods.migrate_v1_to_v2 .
"""
import libcst as cst
from libcst.codemod import CodemodContext, VisitorBasedCodemodCommand
from libcst.codemod.visitors import AddImportsVisitor

class MigrateCompleteV1(VisitorBasedCodemodCommand):
    DESCRIPTION = "Migrate complete_v1(prompt) to complete(messages=[...])"

    def leave_Call(self, original_node, updated_node):
        # Detect: client.complete_v1("text")
        if (isinstance(updated_node.func, cst.Attribute)
                and updated_node.func.attr.value == "complete_v1"
                and len(updated_node.args) == 1):
            prompt_arg = updated_node.args[0].value
            # Rewrite to: client.complete(messages=[{"role": "user", "content": ...}])
            return updated_node.with_changes(
                func=updated_node.func.with_changes(attr=cst.Name("complete")),
                args=[cst.Arg(
                    keyword=cst.Name("messages"),
                    value=cst.parse_expression(
                        f'[{{"role": "user", "content": {prompt_arg.deep_clone().value}}}]'
                    ),
                )],
            )
        return updated_node
Internal shared libraries use semantic versioning strictly: patch for bug/security fixes, minor for new features, major for breaking changes.
until the next major; then a
Consumer services pin to a major range (
) so they receive minor and patch updates automatically while requiring explicit opt-in for breaking major releases.
Deprecations are announced at least one minor release before the major that removes the deprecated API, with explicit migration paths in the warning message and CHANGELOG.
Emergency security patches trigger an automated PR campaign across all consuming repositories; services still on exact pins are flagged as non-compliant.
